verdict_reasoning: EREG (epiregulin) is an EGFR/ErbB family ligand synthesized as a single-pass type I transmembrane proligand (pro-EREG) that transits the plasma membrane before ADAM17-mediated ectodomain shedding releases the soluble active growth factor. The TM precursor stage is real and can mediate juxtacrine signaling, but it is transient and the shed soluble form is the dominant biological actor driving EGFR/ErbB pathway activation. Clinical antibody programs largely target the soluble pool.
